title: No connect-progress feedback in the status bar while a Pod is connecting
description: COSMETIC / feedback bug (investigation-first). While connecting to a Pod (Settings shows 'Remote engine: connecting…' with a CANCEL button), the bottom-left status bar shows nothing — no 'connecting' label, no percentage, no spinner. The user gets zero signal that a connect is in flight from the main surface; only the open Settings panel hints at it. Observed 2026-07-08 on a fresh connect (RTX 4090, EU-RO-1). Suspected regression tied to a recent Pod image build or a status-bar/connect-event wiring change — connect-phase events may no longer reach the status bar, or the status bar stopped rendering the connecting phase. INVESTIGATE: (1) does the connect flow still emit connect-phase progress (remote:connection phase= / a progress event) that the status bar subscribes to? (2) did the status bar (js/shell/statusBar.js) drop or gate the connecting-phase render? (3) is this genuinely new vs always-cosmetic? Compare against the first-boot copy path in shell.js (_pollRemoteReady onSlow StatusBar.notify). LOW priority — cosmetic, connect still works; but bad UX (looks hung on a 90–120s first boot).
